pattern: Once every value's occurrence count is known, the final order is fully determined by a single two-key sort -- frequency ascending, then value descending to break ties -- so counting is the only real work.
hints: First tally how many times each distinct value appears anywhere in the array.
The output order depends only on two things per value: how often it appears, and the value itself for breaking ties.
Sort the array with a key of (count of this value, negative of this value) -- ascending count first, and for equal counts the larger value should come first.

edge_inputs.description: a tie in frequency broken by descending value
mixed positive and negative with several tie groups
min and max value range with a tie
already sorted by frequency ascending, must stay stable in meaning
all elements distinct, every frequency equal to one so pure descending value order

stress.description: random values from a small range so lots of frequency ties occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(-8, 8) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
